name: Service Catalog
description: Grid or list of deployable services with status, region, and operational metadata for cloud and platform consoles.

grid.description: Responsive service card grid for browsing platform services and environments.

slots.toolbar: Optional search, filters, and environment selectors
slots.service-card: Single service tile with icon, name, status, and metadata
slots.service-actions: Contextual actions such as open, deploy, or inspect

list.description: Denser row-based service catalog for operator-heavy pages.

slots.service-row: Single service row with status and actions

visual_brief: Operational service catalog with clean, status-aware cards. Each item should surface the service identity, current health, region or environment, and a few practical facts without reading like a cluttered data table. The grid should feel like a modern control plane, not a marketing gallery.

responsive.mobile: Single-column stack. Metadata wraps cleanly and actions collapse into a single trailing affordance if needed.
responsive.tablet: Two-column grid with enough width for service status, region, and one or two secondary facts.
responsive.desktop: Three-column grid or dense list view depending on operator density. Cards remain easy to scan at a glance.

layout_hints.identity_first: Lead with service name, type icon, and health state. Secondary metadata like region, deployment target, or runtime should support that first read.
layout_hints.operator_density: Avoid stuffing every service property into the card. Prefer a small, repeatable metadata cluster and leave deeper diagnostics for drill-down views.
layout_hints.toolbar_rhythm: If a toolbar is present, keep it visually separate from the grid. Parent workspace layout owns section spacing; the pattern owns only the toolbar-to-grid transition.

motion.micro: Cards may lift or sharpen their border on hover. Status indicators may pulse subtly only when in degraded or deploying states.
motion.transitions: Filtering or sorting should cross-fade or stagger cards in gently without disrupting scanability.

keyboard: Tab should move through toolbar controls, then services, then any row or card actions in reading order.
announcements: Each service should expose name, status, and key metadata semantically so health is not color-only.

composition.ServiceCatalog: Stack(d-section, operator) > [Toolbar?(d-control cluster) + ServiceGrid(d-data, responsive) > ServiceCard[]]
composition.ServiceCard: Card(d-surface, hoverable) > [Identity(icon + name + status) + MetaRow(d-annotation)* + Actions?(d-interactive cluster)]
